Replication of Results from the Paper ’Unified implementation and comparison of Bayesian shrinkage methods for treatment effect estimation in subgroups`

All simulation results from the paper “Unified implementation and comparison of Bayesian shrinkage methods for treatment effect estimation in subgroups” (https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.21967) are stored at https://github.com/openpharma/bonsaiforest2/tree/main/simulations/paper.

This file replicates some of the tables from the paper.

Results: Simulation for a time-to-event endpoint

Standardized overall root mean squared error by simulation scenario

Mean (range) of RMSE, bias, and observed coverage of nominal 95% confidence or credible intervals across subgroups by simulation scenario

TTE: Performance metrics (mean and range) by estimator and scenario

Performance of estimators in subgroup 4a for scenario 2 where the drug does not work

TTE Scenario 2: Performance Metrics for Subgroup 4a (Worst Subgroup, AHR=1.00)
Estimator RMSE Absolute Bias 95% CI Coverage
Global - RHS(τ₀=1, s=2) 0.28 0.19 81%
Global - RHS(τ₀=δplan, s=2) 0.29 0.20 81%
Global - RHS(τ₀=δplan/2, s=2) 0.31 0.24 73%
One-way - HN(φ=1) 0.22 0.09 93%
One-way - HN(φ=δplan) 0.24 0.14 89%
One-way - HN(φ=δplan/2) 0.28 0.22 82%
Population 0.41 0.39 15%
Subgroup 0.20 0.00 95%

Accuracy of correctly identifying the worst subgroup as subgroup 4a for scenario 2

TTE Scenario 2: Accuracy of Identifying Subgroup 4a as the Worst Subgroup
Estimator Replications Correct Identifications Accuracy (%)
Global - RHS(τ₀=1, s=2) 1000 647 65%
Global - RHS(τ₀=δplan, s=2) 1000 649 65%
Global - RHS(τ₀=δplan/2, s=2) 1000 653 65%
One-way - HN(φ=1) 1000 629 63%
One-way - HN(φ=δplan) 1000 653 65%
One-way - HN(φ=δplan/2) 1000 670 67%
Subgroup 1000 507 51%
Accuracy shows the proportion of 1,000 replications where each estimator correctly identified subgroup 4a as the worst (highest AHR/least beneficial treatment effect).

Accuracy of correctly identifying the worst subgroup as subgroup X11cat.a for scenario 3

Continuous Scenario 3: Accuracy of Identifying X11cat level a as the Worst Subgroup
Estimator Replications Correct Identifications Accuracy (%)
Global - Horseshoe(τ₀ =δplan) 1000 893 89%
Global - Horseshoe(τ₀ =δplan/10) 1000 903 90%
Global - Horseshoe(τ₀ =σplan) 1000 895 90%

One-way - HN(φ=σplan) 1000 769 77%
Subgroup 1000 695 70%
Accuracy shows the proportion of 1,000 replications where each estimator correctly identified X11cat=a as the worst (smallest absolute treatment effect - closest to 0).
